enonce.texte:
Read each sentence carefully. Identify whether it is a Type 2 conditional (hypothetical present/unreal now) or a Type 3 conditional (unreal past/regret). Then, complete the verbs in brackets.

Reminder: Type 2 = if + past simple, would + base verb. Type 3 = if + past perfect, would have + past participle.

questions.texte: If I ______ (to be) rich, I ______ (to buy) a school for underprivileged children in my village.
If she ______ (to study) harder last year, she ______ (to pass) her Probatoire examination.
They ______ (to not miss) the bus if they ______ (to wake up) earlier this morning.
questions.reponse: would not have missed / had woken up

sousQuestions.texte: Does this situation refer to the past or the present?

questions.texte: We ______ (to help) you clean the classroom, but we are too tired right now.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete the sentence to form a logical Type 2 conditional: If we ______ (to have) more energy, we ______ (to help) you.

intitule: Regrets and Hypothetical Situations in Real Life

enonce.texte: Read the following real-life problems faced by Cameroonian teenagers. Use the Type 2 (for present/future hypothetical solutions) or Type 3 (for past regrets) conditionals to respond appropriately.

questions.texte: Your friend did not revise for the English test because he played video games all night. He failed the test. Express a regret (Type 3) about this situation.
questions.reponse: If you had not played video games all night, you would have passed the English test. OR If you had revised, you would not have failed.

sousQuestions.texte: Now, give him advice for the next test using a Type 2 conditional.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you revised regularly, you would get better grades.

questions.texte: A student in your school wants to become a doctor, but his family is very poor and cannot pay for medical school. Imagine a hypothetical solution (Type 2) for him.
questions.reponse: If he got a scholarship, he would study medicine. OR If the government reduced school fees, he would become a doctor.

questions.texte: Last week, you witnessed a fight between two students because of a misunderstanding on WhatsApp. You did not intervene. Now you regret it. Use a Type 3 conditional to express your regret.
questions.reponse: If I had intervened, the fight would not have happened. OR If I had spoken to them, they would have resolved the misunderstanding.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the imagined result of your intervention?
sousQuestions.reponse: The fight would have been avoided / They would have made peace.

questions.texte: Many young people in Cameroon are unemployed because they lack professional training. Propose a hypothetical national solution using a Type 2 conditional.
questions.reponse: If the government created more vocational training centers, many young people would find jobs. OR If companies offered paid internships, unemployed youth would gain experience.

enonce.texte: Analyze each statement and answer the questions. Apply Bloom's taxonomy: analyze, evaluate, and create.

questions.texte: Statement: "If we had listened to the school counselor about drug abuse, we would not be in this situation."

sousQuestions.texte: Evaluate: Did the students listen to the counselor? Justify.
sousQuestions.reponse: No, they did not listen. Because the result is negative ("would not be in this situation"), meaning they are currently in a bad situation due to not listening.

sousQuestions.texte: Create a similar sentence of regret about dropping out of school.
sousQuestions.reponse: If I had not dropped out of school in Form 4, I would be preparing for the Probatoire exam now.

questions.texte: Statement: "If I were the Principal of this school, I would ban mobile phones during class."

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze: Is this person the Principal? Why is the verb 'were' used instead of 'was'?
sousQuestions.reponse: No, he/she is not the Principal. In Type 2 conditionals, we use 'were' for all persons (even I/he/she) to show that it is hypothetical/unreal.

sousQuestions.texte: Propose a different hypothetical rule for your school using 'were'.
sousQuestions.reponse: If I were the Discipline Master, I would give community service instead of suspension.
